behaviors.description: Renamed files appear in their own category and are not also counted as a deletion plus an addition.
behaviors.justification: Stage 1 (feature extraction): f_wiring=yes (direct evidence: "appear in their own category and are not also counted as a deletion plus an addition" implies correct handoff from change-type parsing to category counters); f_real_infra=no (can be validated with injected parsed change records); f_model_judgment=no (deterministic classification/counting); f_multi_agentic_steps=no (single-pass assertion). Stage 2 (mapping): not Workflow, not Agentic, not System, f_wiring=yes -> Integration.
behaviors.test_description: Feed a mixed change-set containing at least one rename token plus add/delete/modify tokens into the categorization/report pipeline; assert rename count increments only in renamed bucket and does not increment added/deleted buckets for that file.

behaviors.description: Diffs that include binary files do not cause the tool to crash; binary files are counted in the output.
behaviors.justification: Stage 1 (feature extraction): f_wiring=yes (binary marker must flow to counting/output); f_real_infra=yes (direct evidence: "Diffs that include binary files" and CLI behavior depend on real git/subprocess/file artifacts); f_model_judgment=no (no qualitative model scoring); f_multi_agentic_steps=no. Stage 2 (mapping): not Workflow, not Agentic, f_real_infra=yes -> System.
behaviors.test_description: Create a real temp git repo, commit a binary file change, run CLI via subprocess, assert exit code is success, no crash, and report includes binary file in appropriate count.

behaviors.description: Running the tool on a repository that has no commits yet produces an empty report rather than an error.
behaviors.justification: Stage 1 (feature extraction): f_wiring=yes (empty-state path must reach report renderer); f_real_infra=yes (direct evidence: "repository that has no commits yet" is a real git state and CLI subprocess interaction); f_model_judgment=no; f_multi_agentic_steps=no. Stage 2 (mapping): not Workflow, not Agentic, f_real_infra=yes -> System.
behaviors.test_description: Initialize a fresh git repo with zero commits, run CLI, assert zero exit failure, no exception text, and structured report has zero/empty categories.

behaviors.description: The counts in the report match the actual change history of the repository being analyzed.
behaviors.justification: Stage 1 (feature extraction): f_wiring=yes (count aggregation across categories); f_real_infra=yes (direct evidence: "actual change history of the repository being analyzed" requires real git history/revision traversal); f_model_judgment=no (exact numeric equality); f_multi_agentic_steps=no. Stage 2 (mapping): not Workflow, not Agentic, f_real_infra=yes -> System.
behaviors.test_description: Construct a repo history with known numbers of adds/deletes/renames/modifies across commits, run CLI for target range, and assert report counts exactly equal ground-truth counts computed from git plumbing commands.

behaviors.description: The output is identical whether the changes being analyzed are staged or unstaged.
behaviors.justification: Stage 1 (feature extraction): f_wiring=yes (selection of diff source/index state must map to same report schema); f_real_infra=yes (direct evidence: "staged or unstaged" depends on git index vs working tree states); f_model_judgment=no; f_multi_agentic_steps=no. Stage 2 (mapping): not Workflow, not Agentic, f_real_infra=yes -> System.
behaviors.test_description: In a real repo, prepare equivalent file changes once as unstaged and once staged; run CLI in both conditions and assert byte-for-byte identical structured stdout output.
